title: Morning Reflections - Show Home
body: Home page for the WGSP 1490 AM Saturday show 'Morning Reflections with Rebecca,' airing live 7:00-9:00 AM from the Beale Street studio. Links to episode scripts, guest booking, segment calendar, and sponsor contacts. Audience ~8,000-12,000 weekly listeners.

title: Episode Scripts Archive
body: Archive of weekly show scripts dating back to the 2019 launch. Each script includes opening devotional, guest interview questions, community spotlight segment, and closing reflection. Files are named by air date (e.g., 2026-01-16-script.docx).

title: 2026 Q1 Episode Schedule
body: Episode calendar for January-March 2026. January 3: New Year - 'When the Light Returns' (guest: Rev. Angela Dawson). January 10: Community health literacy. January 17: Honoring the Women of Faith with listener call-ins. January 19: MLK Jr. Day - 'Let Freedom Ring.' February 7: Nurse appreciation - Sandra Coles joins. February 21: Winter reading for the soul. March 7: Radio anniversary special.

title: Guest Booking Notes
body: Tracking sheet for confirmed and prospective guests. Confirmed: Rev. Angela Dawson (Jan 3 New Year episode), Sandra Coles (Feb 7 nurse appreciation), Pastor William Greer (radio anniversary episode, March 2026). Prospective: Lisa Moore (nutrition/wellness), Deacon Charles Mosley (community health fair preview). All guests receive a one-page pre-interview brief. Coordinate logistics with TJ Jackson (tj.jackson@gmail.com).

title: Sponsor Contacts and Invoice Tracker
body: Active sponsors for 'Morning Reflections with Rebecca.' Covenant Insurance Group - annual contract, read live by Rebecca. Mitchell's Flowers - seasonal spot, florist reads own ad. Bluff City Auto Care - 60-sec mid-show spot. Invoices submitted monthly on the 1st. Coordinate with TJ Jackson for billing. All sponsor copy must be reviewed by Rebecca before air.

title: Segment Idea Bank
body: Running list of segment ideas for future episodes. Ideas: 'Grief and the Winter Garden' (grief after a hard year, inspired by Greenline walk conversations). 'Faith on the Night Shift' (honoring healthcare workers, tie-in with Bluff City hospital community). 'Letters to My Younger Self' (listener submissions, read on air). 'Books That Shaped Me' (book club segment, Barbara Kingsolver / Henri Nouwen picks). 'Joy as a Discipline' (fall series). Add new ideas below with source and date.

title: Chaplaincy Resources Home
body: Reference hub for the Bluff City Medical Center pastoral care team. Covers CPE continuing education tracking, policy summaries, mentorship notes for the two CPE residents, and professional development links. Rebecca Price, BCC - Board Certified Chaplain, Pastoral Care, Bluff City Medical Center (since October 2010).

title: CPE Continuing Education Log 2026
body: Annual continuing education tracker. Requirement: 50 hours by December 31, 2026. Completed as of March 2026: 28 hours. Breakdown: APC webinar - Chaplaincy in Oncology Care (4 hrs, Jan 2026). Regional CPE supervisor consultation series (6 hrs, Feb 2026). Trauma-informed spiritual care workshop, Memphis Divinity School (8 hrs, Mar 2026). Self-directed reading - Harold Koenig 'Spirituality in Patient Care' (4 hrs). Peer supervision with Dr. Lorraine Vickers (6 hrs, ongoing). Remaining: 22 hours needed by Dec 31. Planned: Fall APC national conference (12 hrs), plus reading and peer review.

title: CPE Resident Mentorship Notes
body: Notes on the two CPE residents currently under Rebecca's mentorship at Bluff City Medical Center. Resident 1 - strong in active listening; needs coaching on holding space without problem-solving. Resident 2 - excellent written verbatims; boundary-setting in long-term family conferences is a growth edge. Supervision sessions held every other Wednesday. All clinical case details remain in on-site pastoral charting only - this page contains developmental notes only, no patient information.

title: Pastoral Care Policy Quick Reference
body: Quick reference for Bluff City Medical Center pastoral care policies. On-call rotation: one overnight chaplain per week, rotating among 4 staff chaplains. Referrals from nursing come via internal messaging (Epic). Interfaith guidelines: all traditions welcomed; Rebecca's approach: presence over content. ICU family waiting room protocol: check in at nurses' station, introduce yourself and your role, do not initiate prayer unless invited. Palliative care consult: coordinate with Dr. Lorraine Vickers (lorraine.vickers@gmail.com). Annual review due November.

title: Grace Community Church - Event Hub
body: Planning hub for Grace Community Church Memphis events where Rebecca is involved. She delivers meditations, emcees major events, and co-leads the community health fair with Deacon Charles Mosley. Pastoral lead: Pastor William Greer. Sunday service: 8 AM. Sunday dinner: 12 PM. Key upcoming dates tracked in child pages.

title: Community Health Fair 2026 - Planning Notes
body: Annual Grace Community Church health fair planning. Lead organizers: Rebecca Price and Deacon Charles Mosley (charles.mosley@gmail.com). Target date: first Saturday of October 2026 (October 3). Venue: church fellowship hall and parking lot. Planned stations: blood pressure screening, diabetes education (Rebecca coordinates with Lisa Moore), vision screening, nutrition Q&A. Volunteer sign-up sheet posted in church narthex. Rebecca to invite Sandra Coles (nursing professor, Bluff City Community College) to staff the nursing station. Morning Reflections will promote the fair on the two episodes before the event.

title: Epiphany Dawn Service 2026 - Meditation Script
body: Meditation delivered by Rebecca Price at Grace Community Church Epiphany Dawn Service, January 6, 2026, 6:00 AM. Theme: 'Light in the Darkness.' Scripture anchor: Isaiah 60:1-3. Running time: 12 minutes. Opens with a story about morning light on the Cooper-Young porch, moves through grief and waiting, lands on the surprise of new beginnings - a light you didn't earn, didn't plan. Closes with a call to carry that light with you into the new year. Notes: deliver without notes, conversational, alto voice, let the pause after 'arise, shine, for your light has come' breathe.

title: Fall Festival and Trunk-or-Treat 2026 - Emcee Notes
body: Grace Community Church fall festival October 31, 2026. Rebecca emcees. Schedule: 4:00 PM trunk-or-treat opens, 5:00 PM costume contest (kids 12 and under), 5:30 PM community bonfire and music, 6:00 PM closing prayer by Pastor Greer. Rebecca's emcee script includes welcome, transitions, costume contest announcements, and a brief reflection on community and belonging. Coordinate costume contest prizes with Deacon Mosley. Promote on Morning Reflections October 24 episode.

title: Personal - Home and Garden Notes
body: Personal reference notes for the Cooper-Young bungalow. Garden: zinnias, black-eyed Susans, lavender, rosemary planted in back beds. Bird feeders filled weekly with black-oil sunflower seed. Next garden project: expand lavender border along the south fence once it warms. Porch swing cushion replaced March 2026. Hardwood floors - use Murphy's Oil Soap (diluted). Mercy the cat - annual vet visit due November 2026, Dr. Patton at Midtown Animal Clinic.

title: Book Notes - Current Reading
body: Running notes on books in progress. Currently reading: 'The Poisonwood Bible' by Barbara Kingsolver (re-read for possible book segment on Morning Reflections - theme: faith under pressure). Also reading: 'The Ruthless Elimination of Hurry' by John Mark Comer (practical; aligns with compassion fatigue work). On deck: 'Beguiled by Beauty' by Marilyn Chandler McEntyre, and a re-read of Henri Nouwen's 'The Wounded Healer' before mentoring a CPE resident who asked about it. Picked up from Midtown Pages.

title: Meal Prep and Weekly Rhythm Notes
body: Weekly rhythm reminders. Meal prep: Monday and Wednesday evenings. Monday staples: grilled chicken, roasted broccoli, brown rice, veggie soup batch. Wednesday: fish tacos with slaw, overnight oats for Thu/Fri. Sunday: pot roast (slow cooker on before church), mac and cheese, cornbread. Kroger run typically Friday after visiting Dorothy. Grocery budget ~$450/month. No alcohol in the house. Sparkling water (HEB brand), sweet tea for guests.

title: Dorothy - Caregiver Notes
body: Care coordination notes for Dorothy Price (DOB March 8, 1950), South Memphis. Rebecca visits every Friday at 4:30 PM with groceries. Monthly: med refills coordinated on the 1st. Support budget: $350/month. Emergency contacts on Dorothy's fridge: Rebecca (901) 555-0xxx primary, Brenda Price-Hall (601) 555-0412. Health matters discussed only with Naomi Price (naomi.price@gmail.com) and Brenda Price-Hall (brenda.pricehall@gmail.com). No health details stored here - kept in private journal only.
